images.caption:
Figure 1.Qualitative examples and performance comparison.
(a)SpotSoundaccurately grounds timestamps relevant to the query.
(b)SpotSoundidentifies sound events described in the query that are absent from the audio.
(c) Quantitative comparison;bluedenotesSpotSound, andreddenotes previous top-tier models. We evaluate models on four benchmarks and report the mIoU and R1@.5 for each.

Figure 2.Model architecture and dataset generation pipeline.
(a) InSpotSound, we construct an interleaved sequence of timestamps and audio tokens and concatenate with a language query, and feed it to the LLM to predict the target temporal interval.
(b) We employ LLMs to generate captions for foreground audio, then randomly mix foreground and background sounds to synthesise the final audio, preserving the insertion timestamp as ground-truth.

Figure 3.Qualitative comparison with other large audio-language models. (a) Example cases from theSpotSound-Bench, illustrating the temporal windows predicted by each model in response to the textual query. (b) Examples drawn from the Clotho-Moment, demonstrating cases where the models identify the non-existent sound events.
Figure S1.Category distributions for the synthetic dataset.
Figure S2.Duration and window length distributions forSpotSound-Bench.
Figure S3.Success case and failure case fromSpotSound-Bench.
Figure S4.Success case and failure case from AudioGrounding.
Figure S5.Success case and failure case from UnAV-100 subset.
